rows.query: List the S4 methods dispatching on class GeneModel
rows.grepVerdict: FEASIBLE-COSTLY (raw setMethod hits, agent must parse which dispatch on GeneModel; no machine-typed result)
rows.lspVerdict: CANNOT-STRUCTURAL (no LSP 3.17 request encodes dispatch-table membership for a class; callHierarchy/incomingCalls finds callers of a generic, not setMethod registrations)

rows.query: What script does Snakemake rule fit_model run?
rows.grepVerdict: FEASIBLE (find the rule, then read its script: directive — 2 passes, unstructured)
rows.lspVerdict: CANNOT-STRUCTURAL (LSP is language-scoped; no request bridges .smk → the .R it invokes)

rows.query: From rule fit_model, reach the S4 dispatch method across the process boundary
rows.grepVerdict: CANNOT-PROVE-COMPOSITION (grep can spot-check each hop independently but cannot prove they share nodes — a crossLang landing in a different file than the S4 class owner would pass every per-hop grep)
rows.lspVerdict: CANNOT-STRUCTURAL (cross-language process boundary + S4 dispatch registry — no request covers either)

rows.query: What R template does Nextflow process PREDICT run?
rows.grepVerdict: FEASIBLE-COSTLY (find process, read its template directive, resolve templates/ convention)
rows.lspVerdict: CANNOT-STRUCTURAL (cross-language .nf → .R, no LSP request crosses it)

rows.query: What script does the Python function run_analysis shell out to?
rows.grepVerdict: FEASIBLE (the call site is greppable, but the py→R link is text the agent must resolve; no edge to traverse onward)
rows.lspVerdict: CANNOT-STRUCTURAL (a Python LSP does not resolve a subprocess argument into the R file it names)

rows.query: Where is the function render defined?

rows.owAnswer: partial — symbol index is not full-text
rows.grepVerdict: GREP-WINS (raw text presence, zero indexing overhead — grep is the right tool)
